title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA demonstrated real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce diffusion models from 50 steps to as few as one. The technique stacks three layers: quantization with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2, skip-caching that avoids recomputing unchanged latent chunks, and distillation using either trajectory-based or distribution-based student training approaches.
writeup: Ziv Ilan of NVIDIA presents how his team ran real-time video diffusion on the Blackwell B200, cutting diffusion models from 50 steps down to as few as one. The method stacks three techniques: dynamic quantization (developed with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2), skip-caching that avoids recomputing unchanged latent chunks, and step distillation using either trajectory-based or distribution-based student training. The talk matters because compounding these layers attacks the per-step cost of diffusion inference rather than relying on a single optimization. The concrete takeaway: quantization, latent caching, and distillation are complementary and combine to compress the iterative sampling loop dramatically.
